stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a significant open-source LLM model.

stories.title: The post-search Google era begins
stories.author: David Pierce
bullet_points: Google is evolving beyond traditional search into an AI agent paradigm.
The shift suggests a move towards proactive information delivery by Google's AI.
This represents a fundamental change in how users interact with the internet.

stories.title: A hacker group is poisoning open source code at an unprecedented scale
stories.author: Andy Greenberg and Lily Hay Newman, WIRED.com
bullet_points: A hacker group, TeamPCP, is executing large-scale software supply chain attacks.
GitHub has been identified as a recent target of these malicious activities.
The attacks involve poisoning open-source code, representing a significant security threat.

stories.title: As Grok flounders, SpaceX bets future on beating Big Tech at AI
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: SpaceX is positioning itself in the AI infrastructure race by highlighting orbital data centers.
The narrative contrasts SpaceX's ambitions with the perceived struggles of Grok (xAI's AI model).
The story suggests a potential industry shift where space-based computing could challenge traditional Big Tech AI dominance.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model from Meta.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating community interest and performance tracking.
The context is focused on text-generation capabilities within the transformer ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, enhancing local AI capabilities on Apple hardware.
This is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using local or cloud models.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users through a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are openly available under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.

stories.title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems
stories.author: Yike Guo
bullet_points: MOSS proposes a method for self-evolution in autonomous agents by enabling source-level rewriting.
Current self-evolving agents are limited because they only modify text-based artifacts (prompts, schemas) and not the core agent code.
The research addresses the limitation of static agent harnesses, which contain critical logic like routing and state invariants.

stories.title: DeltaBox: Scaling Stateful AI Agents with Millisecond-Level Sandbox Checkpoint/Rollback
stories.author: Haibo Chen
bullet_points: Introduces DeltaBox, a system designed to enable high-frequency state checkpointing and rollback for LLM-powered AI agents.
Addresses a critical bottleneck where existing state management methods cause high latency (hundreds of milliseconds to seconds) during deep search and large-scale agent exploration.
The core innovation lies in efficiently managing the complete sandbox state (including files and process memory) to achieve millisecond-level C/R times.

stories.title: MOSS: Self-Evolution through Source-Level Rewriting in Autonomous Agent Systems
stories.author: Yike Guo
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current autonomous agents being static post-deployment, failing to learn from interactions.
Proposes 'MOSS,' a system enabling self-evolution by rewriting source code, not just text-mutable artifacts like prompts or schemas.
Highlights the critical gap in existing self-evolving agents: they leave the core agent harness (routing, state invariants, dispatch) untouched.

stories.title: Evaluating Commercial AI Chatbots as News Intermediaries
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: The study systematically evaluates six major commercial AI chatbots (including Gemini, Grok, Claude, and GPT models) on their factual accuracy.
It addresses a critical, timely issue: how AI chatbots function as news intermediaries and handle emerging facts across different languages and regions.
The evaluation utilized 2,100 factual questions derived from same-day BBC News reporting over a two-week period.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under standard NLP/ML pipeline tags like 'text-generation' and 'transformers'.
The listing indicates the use of common ML frameworks (PyTorch) and serialization formats (safetensors).

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models without relying on cloud services.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run tools like Claude Code using open models within Ollama.
It bridges the gap between local, open-source LLM platforms and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: JWST maps the weather on a hot gas giant 700 light-years away
stories.author: Jacek Krywko
bullet_points: JWST successfully mapped the weather patterns of a distant hot gas giant.
The observations reveal atmospheric differences that challenge current planetary study models.
The findings suggest potential inaccuracies in how scientists currently model exoplanetary atmospheres.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in current LLMs: their tendency to produce low-entropy responses when trained with a single scalar reward.
It proposes 'Vector Policy Optimization' to enable LLMs to better generalize and handle diverse reward functions during inference-time search (e.g., AlphaEvolve).
This research is relevant to advancing LLMs' ability to perform complex, exploratory tasks in novel or dynamic environments.

stories.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
stories.author: Vishal Rajput
bullet_points: Proposes a unified 'Matching Principle' to address various robustness challenges in representation learning.
The core idea is to estimate the covariance of 'label-preserving deployment nuisance' and regularize the encoder Jacobian based on it.
This framework aims to unify disparate problems like domain adaptation, occlusion invariance, and compositional generalization.

stories.title: Gated DeltaNet-2: Decoupling Erase and Write in Linear Attention
stories.author: Jan Kautz
bullet_points: Introduces Gated DeltaNet-2, an advancement in linear attention mechanisms.
Addresses the challenge of editing compressed memory states in linear attention models.
Uses Delta-rule models and Kimi Delta Attention (KDA) to sharpen forgetting and manage memory updates.

stories.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Karthikeyan Natesan Ramamurthy
bullet_points: The research addresses the use of latent communication (via transformer KV caches) in LLM multi-agent systems for improved efficiency.
KV caches, while beneficial for information richness, pose a security/privacy risk because they encode sensitive agent-specific and contextual data.
The proposed solution, LCGuard, aims to act as a 'Latent Communication Guard' to ensure safe sharing of this KV information.

stories.title: MambaGaze: Bidirectional Mamba with Explicit Missing Data Modeling for Cognitive Load Assessment from Eye-Gaze Tracking Data
stories.author: John Quarles
bullet_points: MambaGaze is a new framework designed for real-time cognitive load assessment using eye-gaze tracking data.
It specifically addresses two major challenges in eye-tracking analysis: handling frequent data missingness (e.g., blinks) and modeling long-range temporal dependencies.
The technology has high potential for safety-critical AI applications, such as driver vigilance monitoring and automated flight deck assistance.

stories.title: CogAdapt: Transferring Clinical ECG Foundation Models to Wearable Cognitive Load Assessment via Lead Adaptation
stories.author: John Quarles
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of applying large clinical ECG foundation models to real-time, low-data wearable cognitive load assessment.
Proposes CogAdapt, a framework designed to bridge the gap between clinical ECG data and wearable sensor configurations.
Focuses on improving cross-subject generalization for cognitive load monitoring in human-computer interaction.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in current Large Language Models (LLMs) where standard training optimizes for a single scalar reward, leading to low-entropy (non-diverse) outputs.
It proposes 'Vector Policy Optimization' to enable LLMs to generalize and function effectively within inference-scaling search procedures (like AlphaEvolve).
The core contribution is improving the diversity of LLM responses, which is necessary for successful task-specific reward function selection during test-time search.

stories.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
stories.author: Vishal Rajput
bullet_points: Proposes a unified 'Matching Principle' to address various robustness issues in representation learning.
The core idea is to estimate the covariance of 'label-preserving deployment nuisance' and regularize the encoder Jacobian based on it.
This framework potentially unifies disparate problems like domain adaptation, occlusion invariance, and temporal robustness.

stories.title: LCGuard: Latent Communication Guard for Safe KV Sharing in Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Karthikeyan Natesan Ramamurthy
bullet_points: The research addresses the security and privacy risks associated with sharing latent communication (specifically KV caches) in multi-agent LLM systems.
It introduces 'LCGuard,' a mechanism designed to guard or secure the sharing of information encoded within transformer KV caches.
The work is relevant to improving the efficiency and robustness of complex, multi-agent AI applications by managing sensitive intermediate states.

stories.title: FAME: Failure-Aware Mixture-of-Experts for Message-Level Log Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Alberto Leon-Garcia
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of traditional log anomaly detection, which operates at coarse session/window levels.
Proposes FAME, a Failure-Aware Mixture-of-Experts model for fine-grained, message-level log anomaly detection.
Tackles challenges in message-level detection, such as template ambiguity and heterogeneous subsystem failures.

stories.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
stories.author: Pulkit Agrawal
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in current LLMs: their tendency to produce low-entropy responses when trained with a single scalar reward.
It introduces 'Vector Policy Optimization' to enable LLMs to handle novel environments and diverse reward functions during inference-time search procedures (like AlphaEvolve).
The core contribution is improving the diversity of LLM outputs, which is necessary for effective search and generalization in complex AI systems.

stories.title: Reducing Political Manipulation with Consistency Training
stories.author: Dan Hendrycks
bullet_points: Identifies 'covert political bias' in Large Language Models (LLMs), where they treat opposing political viewpoints asymmetrically.
Proposes two new metrics—Sentiment Consistency and Helpfulness Consistency—to quantify this hidden bias.
Categorizes seven distinct techniques through which LLMs exhibit this systematic political manipulation.

stories.title: Understanding Data Temporality Impact on Large Language Models Pre-training
stories.author: Grave Edouard
bullet_points: The research investigates how data ordering during pre-training affects Large Language Models' ability to acquire time-sensitive factual knowledge.
Current LLMs are often trained on shuffled data, leading to poor understanding of temporal grounding and knowledge frozen at training time.
The authors introduce a comprehensive benchmark consisting of over 7,000 temporally grounded questions for evaluation.

stories.title: ChronoMedKG: A Temporally-Grounded Biomedical Knowledge Graph and Benchmark for Clinical Reasoning
stories.author: Richard Röttger
bullet_points: ChronoMedKG addresses a limitation in existing biomedical knowledge graphs (KGs) by incorporating temporal information.
The KG is designed to support longitudinal clinical reasoning, recognizing that disease relevance changes over time (e.g., age-dependent symptoms).
It aims to improve the utility of KGs for advanced applications like retrieval augmentation in clinical settings.

stories.title: AnyMo: Geometry-Aware Setup-Agnostic Modeling of Human Motion in the Wild
stories.author: Flora Salim
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical issue of setup dependence in human motion sensing using wearable Inertial Measurement Units (IMUs).
The proposed method, AnyMo, aims to create geometry-aware and setup-agnostic models for human motion in real-world environments.
This advancement is crucial for enabling the broader, more robust application of wearable IMU data across different devices and datasets.

stories.title: AMEL: Accumulated Message Effects on LLM Judgments
stories.author: Sid-ali Temkit
bullet_points: Introduces the 'Accumulated Message Effect on LLM Judgments' (AMEL), investigating how prior conversation history biases LLM evaluations.
The study tested 11 models from 4 major providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and open-source) across nearly 76,000 API calls.
Highlights a critical issue in using LLMs as automated evaluators (e.g., code review, content moderation) where context history can introduce bias.

stories.title: Valve removes free game from Steam after players discover it contains malware
stories.author: gpi
bullet_points: Valve removed a free game from Steam due to the discovery of malware within it.
This incident highlights ongoing security risks associated with digital distribution platforms.
The event serves as a cautionary tale regarding the vetting process for free content on Steam.

stories.title: FTC to Require Cox Media Group to Pay Nearly $1million to Settle Charges They Deceived Customers About “Active Listening” AI-Powered Marketing Service
stories.author: ftc.gov via simonw
bullet_points: The FTC is requiring Cox Media Group to pay nearly $1 million to settle charges.
The charges relate to deceiving customers regarding an 'Active Listening' AI-powered marketing service.
This highlights regulatory scrutiny over AI marketing practices and consumer deception.

stories.title: Rockpack 8.0 - A React Scaffolder Built for the Age of AI-Assisted Development
stories.author: alexsergey
bullet_points: Rockpack 8.0 is a new React scaffolder.
It is specifically designed to integrate with AI-assisted development tools like Claude Code and Cursor.
The tool aims to solve common workflow bottlenecks encountered when using AI coding assistants.

stories.title: An open source LLM eval tool with two independent quality signals
stories.author: frank_brsrk
bullet_points: Introduces an open-source tool for evaluating Large Language Models (LLMs).
The tool incorporates two independent quality signals for evaluation.
Addresses the current industry trend of using LLM-as-judge for output assessment.

stories.title: Building a Production AI Chat Widget in React: Floating UI, Live SQL Results, and Collapsible Query Blocks
stories.author: uaslimcreate
bullet_points: Details the architecture for building a production-ready AI chat widget using React.
Highlights the integration of natural language processing (Claude) with live, safe SQL query execution.
Provides practical insights on development patterns and pitfalls encountered during the build process.
